div1.entryFree:
Shave (partic. --d; attributively --n), 1) to strip of the hair or beard: s. the head, Meas. IV, 2, 187. the --n Hercules, Ado III, 3, 145. Bardolph was --d, H4A III, 3, 68. I'll s. your crown, H6B II, 1, 51.
2) to cut off with a razor: were I the wearer of Antonius' beard, I would not s. it, Ant. II, 2, 8.
